hypothesis: If a small instruction-tuned LLM is continually pre-trained on MOF-specific literature plus large-scale chemical compound corpora before supervised fine-tuning on QMOF and hMOF using MOFid text inputs, then it will achieve lower MAE on band gap and gas adsorption predictions than both structure-based baselines and the same model without such pre-training, with the largest gains under limited (1–10k) SFT data, because domain pre-training encodes transferable chemical knowledge from text.
